item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image could not be properly sourced and is assumed to be non-compliant. As such, it has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
anteristing irtecle

EDIT: OOOOOHHHHH, I get it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the concept, but it could have been better explored, Maybe a table of contents with spooky examples of those strange individuals. Most of the article's cool points were too far ahead in it, and felt difficult to get to. Neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Why I Left Harry's All-Night Hamburgers", by Lawrence Watt-Evans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting article, interesting article, anteristing irtecle.

Upvoting, and it reminds me of that one sci-fi story, which I sadly cannot place a name to. Read that story so many times in English classes over the years, though. The one with the restaurant, and the guy on night-shift, and the weird aliens and crap that came by every night. Kinda reminded me of that. I'm rambling now, I should stop rambling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Orifices filled with toxic or reactive gases

Orifices are openings, so this should be something like "Bodily cavities filled with …".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foundation personnel are allowed to provide unsatisfactory service for the purpose of discouraging local business.

yes

A Waffle House enthusiast friend of mine is going to wake to find this link in his inbox. I could stand to see more SCPs with bizarre procedures like this. "But I went to gradschool to AVOID working in the food services…"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kind of don't see why this is here? It's like, it makes you randomly terrified?

This one actually has a bit of real science behind it; people who spend prolonged periods amid unshielded and variable EM fields tend to display symptoms of paranoia, nervousness, and even mild hallucinations. This usually only ends up happening in enclosed areas (i.e. a closet with a large improperly shielded circuit breaker, a very small room with entirely too many computer systems, etc.) and usually amongst people with a certain sensitivity to it, but it's been known to happen in some larger areas from time to time. The fact that this place causes electrical malfunctions when the events happen actually plays along with it.

Not sure if it was INTENTIONAL or not, but even accidental reason is better than no reason XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this, yet sort of felt like "And then what?" at the end. I realize that's sort of irrational - it makes people with weird organs pop up! But it felt like the SCP was building up to something more, that when these extra people with extra organs showed up I hadn't quite arrived at the point. I can't tell you why I thought that though. Maybe I was expecting something more like scp-453, with the "events". I'm not really holding that against you (I upvoted) but I thought I'd share it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's… just lazy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank god this isn't a Huddle House. The poor service and food wouldn't be noticed. It's telling that Decatur, GA is the site of Huddle House Headquarters (HHHQ), and yet doesn't have a single Huddle House.

Also, I now imagine that this is where 1377's come from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Octodad is normal manpersons!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually yeah, between Goodwill and Voct's interpretations I don't think I will ever share my own personal thoughts because these are pretty awesome interpretations that fit well with the implied backstories of other things I've written.

I might still write a "non-canon" tale about this place though. The thing I wanted to include even more than an alien patron calling the place "disgusting" but I could not find a good way to work it in… Do you guys know what Waffle House's slogan is? Right at the top of their website?

Under the Yellow Sign.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, poop. Sorry. I'll undo that, then.

Steve will always have a place in my heart, though, as will this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a beautiful interpretation and is now my head-canon for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Imagine that you're making a wildlife documentary. You want lots of footage, so you go into the animal's native environment and set up a food dispenser.

You come back a few days later, and you've got some great footage, but the dispenser has been knocked over. So you set up another dispenser on the same site.

Later, you decide that you can get even better footage if you make some replicas of the animals and send them out to mingle with the others. What should your replicas look like? …. eh, just duplicate the precise external appearance of the ones who come to the dispenser. The animals won't notice! And the fact that all the internal details are wrong… that obviously doesn't matter either, since these aren't meant to actually be members of the species.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Task Force Gamma-5 "Waffle Irons"

That is all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foundation personnel are allowed to provide unsatisfactory

I would change "allowed" to "encouraged." Really hammer home the point that repeat customer's are discouraged.

Otherwise, big fan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think some extradimensional species is using this as a "discrete" entrance into our universe. Humans love pancakes, right? They won't think anything bad about a pancake restaurant, of course!

They examine people that enter the restaurant in order to make convincing disguises, perhaps not possessing enough genetic variation in their species to realize that identical human beings aren't all that common…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
downvoted for malicious upvoting!!!

(thank you but any silly notes I hide in the history shouldn't be a thing to vote for :3c)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dang it. I wasn't going to upvote. Then I saw the history. Dang it.

I like the concept, but it seems a little longer and not as clear as it could be. Not bad, but I think it could be tidied up a little.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted at "a pattern of poor service keeps most locals away", but oh man that addendum. I like that this article is pretty simple, too, as effective as it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Up-voted based on title alone.
The article gave me no reason to change my mind. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because their waffles are just that good.

Well, they're okay. I mean, they're not that bad. Actually, they're kinda pretty terrible.

Side note: my dad is a dentist and when he was practicing, his clinic was about 3 miles from a Waffle House, so the employees knew about him and would frequently use him as their dentist since he was so close by. Out of a staff of about 10-15 people, they collectively had enough teeth to fill 2 mouths.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure you even need the last addendum, since a restaurant that rebuilds itself and creates/teleports people into itself is pretty weird. Do they have to be missing organs or have glass in their stomachs? I don't think so, but that's just my opinion. I still like it, so upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God damn you, Sorts.

…

The bargain is sealed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dig it. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the local populace no longer appears at the location by extra-ordinary means

Were they being teleported into the restaurant? Might they not mention that more directly, since it means people from outside the containment zone could suddenly appear inside it? Meaning, the people who were with that person might wonder where they went?

I wonder how big the "Oh, that place is still open. That seems normal" effect is. Would someone who sees it revert notice the change? Or would they (like those inside) accept the reversion?

I mean, just cause it's not happening now doesn't mean it won't start up again without warning, right?

after all previous containment and demolition attempts failed.

Imagining the construction crew showing up the next day makes me laugh.

Bob: "Didn't we blow this place up?"
Steve: "Yeah, pretty sure we did."
Bob: (pause) "I'm getting breakfast."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
postmortem investigation typically finds severe internal abnormalities and toxic substances, including but not limited to:

Everything on the list made me squirm. Oh, extraterrestrial life…

Love the concept. And all the steves made me laugh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
appear have been contained

Typo

Also, an extra dimensional waffle house that aliens use as a stopping station? Upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bit busy can't do a review but there are some points that bothered me plus I didn't really understand it?

Will come back when less busy to do something more thorough.

EDIT:

a strong feeling of paranoia and apprehension

I kind of don't see why this is here? It's like, it makes you randomly terrified?

our reality

I don't approve of 'our' in a document. I'd almost thing it would be better as 'this' or something that does not make me eerugh. Of course this is my personal quibble, I just feel it breaks tone.

You mostly fixed the other things I had issue with, but I still don't get it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: because it has tentacles and stuff man /scienceanswer

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Extra-terrestrial symbiotic life" ?

How do we know it's extraterrestrial?
